entry.title: Ex-L3Harris executive sentenced to 87 months in prison for selling zero-day exploits to Russian broker
entry.content:
Additionally, the U.S. Treasury sanctioned the Russian zero-day brokerage that Peter Williams sold the exploits to. The post Ex-L3Harris executive sentenced to 87 months in prison for selling zero-day exploits to Russian broker appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Vulnerabilities grew like weeds in 2025, but only 1% were weaponized in attacks
entry.content:
Too many defenders and researchers are paying attention to defects and unsubstantiated exploit concepts that aren’t worth their time, VulnCheck’s Caitlin Condon said. The post Vulnerabilities grew like weeds in 2025, but only 1% were weaponized in attacks appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Governments issue warning over Cisco zero-day attacks dating back to 2023
entry.content:
The global campaign marks the second series of multiple actively exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in Cisco edge technology since last spring. The similarities don’t end there. The post Governments issue warning over Cisco zero-day attacks dating back to 2023 appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Senate moves one step closer to passing health care cyber reforms
entry.content:
A legislative package that would overhaul cybersecurity practices at the Department of Health and Human Services sailed through committee. The post Senate moves one step closer to passing health care cyber reforms appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Iran's MuddyWater Targets Orgs With Fresh Malware as Tensions Mount
entry.content:
The long-active Iranian threat group debuted various attack strains and payloads in attacks against organizations in the Middle East and Africa.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Spitting Cash: ATM Jackpotting Attacks Surged in 2025
entry.content:
The attacks cost banks more than $20 million in losses last year, as criminals used many of the same tools and tactics they have wielded for more than a decade.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: As Cybersecurity Firms Chase AI, VC Market Skyrockets
entry.content:
Investments in cybersecurity startups took off in 2025 as venture capital firms focused not just on AI-native tech but on talent as well.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Lazarus Group Picks a New Poison: Medusa Ransomware
entry.content:
The North Korean threat group also leveraged Comebacker backdoor, Blindingcan RAT, and info stealer Infohook in its recent attacks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Attackers Now Need Just 29 Minutes to Own a Network
entry.content:
Credential misuse, AI tools, and security blind spots help attackers move through breached networks faster than ever, CrowdStrike finds.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: 'Richter Scale' Model Measures Magnitude of OT Cyber Incidents
entry.content:
ICS/OT experts have devised a scoring system for rating the severity and effects of cybersecurity events in operational technology environments.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: PCI Council Says Threats to Payments Systems Are Speeding Up
entry.content:
The PCI Security Standards Council experienced a record year in many regards, but its first annual report shows it needs to work even faster to stay ahead of attackers.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: RAMP Forum Seizure Fractures Ransomware Ecosystem
entry.content:
Researchers suggest defenders monitor how these malicious groups re-form and leverage the useful threat intel to guide their next moves.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cisco SD-WAN Zero-Day Under Exploitation for 3 Years
entry.content:
The maximum-severity vulnerability CVE-2026-20127 was exploited by an unknown but sophisticated threat actor who left very little evidence behind.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Marquis v. SonicWall Lawsuit Ups the Breach Blame Game
entry.content:
When a company gets breached through a third-party security vendor, who should bear responsibility? For one FinTech company, the answer is the firewall provider.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Claude Code Security Shows Promise, Not Perfection
entry.content:
Claude Code's introduction rippled across the stock market, but researchers and analysts say its impact was overstated, as they peel back the layers.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: The Case for Why Better Breach Transparency Matters
entry.content:
It's become a standard practice for organizations to disclose the bare minimum about a data breach, or worse — not disclose the incident at all.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Life Mirrors Art: Ransomware Hits Hospitals on TV & IRL
entry.content:
HBO's "The Pitt" is showing audiences what a real Mississippi healthcare system is going through this week, thanks to a ransomware attack.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Critical Flaws Exposed Gardyn Smart Gardens to Remote Hacking
entry.content:
CISA has released an advisory to warn about four vulnerabilities discovered by a researcher in Gardyn Home and Gardyn Studio. The post Critical Flaws Exposed Gardyn Smart Gardens to Remote Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Juniper Networks PTX Routers Affected by Critical Vulnerability
entry.content:
An out-of-band security update for Junos OS Evolved patches the remote code execution vulnerability CVE-2026-21902. The post Juniper Networks PTX Routers Affected by Critical Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: 38 Million Allegedly Impacted by ManoMano Data Breach
entry.content:
Hackers stole personal information such as names, email addresses, phone numbers, and other information. The post 38 Million Allegedly Impacted by ManoMano Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: In Other News: ATT&CK Advisory Council, Russian Cyberattacks Aid Missile Strikes, Predator Bypasses iOS Indicators
entry.content:
Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: cyber valuations surge, OpenAI disrupts malicious AI use, ShinyHunters claims Odido breach. The post In Other News: ATT&CK Advisory Council, Russian Cyberattacks Aid Missile Strikes, Predator Bypasses iOS Indicators appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Canadian Tire Data Breach Impacts 38 Million Accounts
entry.content:
Names, addresses, email addresses, phone numbers, and encrypted passwords were compromised in the attack. The post Canadian Tire Data Breach Impacts 38 Million Accounts appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Wyden blocks Rudd confirmation to lead Cyber Command, NSA
entry.content:
Rudd “does not have the background that would allow him to immediately step into” the role of leading Cyber Command, Wyden wrote in a letter that was included in the Congressional Record on Wednesday.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Former Air Force officer arrested for conspiring with hacker to provide flight training to Chinese military
entry.content:
Gerald Eddie Brown, 65, was arrested in Jeffersonville, Indiana on Thursday after spending nearly three years living in China and allegedly providing combat aircraft training to pilots in the Chinese Air Force.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
entry.content:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Unknown
entry.content:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Medical Device Manufacturer UFP Technologies Confirms Data Stolen in Cyberattack
entry.content:
The U.S. medical device manufacturer UFP Technologies has submitted a FORM 8-K filing to the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) […] The post Medical Device Manufacturer UFP Technologies Confirms Data Stolen in Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Healthcare Data Breach Statistics – Updated for 2026
entry.content:
The HIPAA Journal has compiled healthcare data breach statistics from October 2009, when the Department of Health and Human Services […] The post Healthcare Data Breach Statistics – Updated for 2026 appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Center for Advanced Eye Care; Southwest C.A.R.E Center; Evergreen Healthcare Group Announce Data Breaches
entry.content:

📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.content:
Cyberattacks and data breaches have been announced by the healthcare providers Carolina Foot & Ankle Associates, New Age Dermatology, and […] The post Carolina Foot & Ankle Associates Notifies Patients About December 2025 Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Rebound Orthopedics & Neurosurgery Pays $2.5 Million to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit
entry.content:
Rebound Orthopedics & Neurosurgery, a Vancouver, WA-based orthopedic and neurosurgery practice, has agreed to pay $2,500,000 to settle a class action […] The post Rebound Orthopedics & Neurosurgery Pays $2.5 Million to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Soaring Insider Breach Costs Driven by Shadow AI Use
entry.content:
On average, businesses with 500 or more employees are losing an average of $19.5 million a year due to insider […] The post Soaring Insider Breach Costs Driven by Shadow AI Use appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Ransomware payment rate drops to record low as attacks surge
entry.content:
The number of ransomware victims paying threat actors has dropped to 28% last year, an all-time low, despite a significant increase in the number of claimed attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Olympique Marseille confirms 'attempted' cyberattack after data leak
entry.content:
French professional football club Olympique de Marseille has confirmed a cyberattack after a threat actor claimed on Monday that it breached the club's systems earlier this month. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: European DYI chain ManoMano data breach impacts 38 million customers
entry.content:
DIY store chain ManoMano is notifying customers of a data breach personal data, which was caused by hackers compromising a third-party service provider. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Europol-led crackdown on The Com hackers leads to 30 arrests
entry.content:
A yearlong Europol-coordinated operation dubbed "Project Compass" has led to 30 arrests and 179 suspects being tied to "The Com," an online cybercrime collective that targets children and teenagers. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: APT37 hackers use new malware to breach air-gapped networks
entry.content:
North Korean hackers are deploying newly uncovered tools to move data between internet-connected and air-gapped systems, spread via removable drives, and conduct covert surveillance. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: QuickLens Chrome extension steals crypto, shows ClickFix attack
entry.content:
A Chrome extension named "QuickLens - Search Screen with Google Lens" has been removed from the Chrome Web Store after it was compromised to push malware and attempt to steal crypto from thousands of users. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: UK Court of Appeal Rules on the Concept of Personal Data in the Context of Data Security
entry.content:
Sam Jungyun Choi, Jadzia Pierce, and Paul Maynard of Covington and Burling write: On February 19, 2026, the UK Court of Appeal handed down its decision in DSG Retail Limited v The Information Commissioner [2026] EWCA Civ 140. The Court ruled that a controller’s data security duty applies to all personal data for which it acts as... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Hacker Used Anthropic’s Claude to Steal Sensitive Mexican Data
entry.content:
Andrew Martin and Caroline Millan report: A hacker exploited Anthropic PBC’s artificial intelligence chatbot to carry out a series of attacks against Mexican government agencies, resulting in the theft of a huge trove of sensitive tax and voter information, according to cybersecurity researchers. The unknown Claude user wrote Spanish-language prompts for the chatbot to act as... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: South Korean Authorities Accidentally Hand Hackers $4.8M in Crypto
entry.content:
Kenrodgers Fabian reports: A security blunder hit South Korea as the National Tax Service accidentally exposed a crypto wallet’s recovery key, leading to a $4.8 million theft. The incident happened when the tax authority published a press release photo showing the mnemonic phrase for a seized wallet. This phrase, essentially the master password for virtual... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Former Nuance employee admits breaching more than 1.2M Geisinger patient records
entry.content:
John Beauge reports the latest update in the case of Max Vance, also known as Andre J. Burk. Vance had been employed by Nuance Communications, a business associate of Geisinger Health. After his employment was terminated, he was still able to access Geisinger patient data. Geisinger detected the breach and notified Nuance. Now Beauge reports:... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Hackers steal medical details of 15 million in France
entry.content:
Third-party vendors/business associates continue to be responsible for huge breaches involving patient data. Rébecca Frasquet and Chloé Rabs of AFP report: France’s health ministry said Friday that administrative details and medical notes on more than 15 million people had been hacked. The announcement came only days after officials warned that the details of 1.2 million... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: KT, LG Uplus face lingering fallout over hacking incidents
entry.content:
Lee Gyu-lee reports: Korea’s two major telecom companies, KT and LG Uplus, continue to grapple with the fallout over hacking incidents and data breaches that triggered customer departures and mounting pressure for tougher sanctions. KT has extended the deadline for customers to apply for early termination fee refunds after complaints that some users were unable... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Leaked Odido data exposes sensitive information
entry.content:
In contrast to entities and courts that try to chill reporting, look at coverage of the Odido breach, where Dutch news outlets are not censored from informing the public about the scope of the breach. As seen on NL Times: A second batch of stolen customer data from Dutch telecom company Odido has revealed highly sensitive information... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Israel plunges Iran into darkness with largest cyberattack in history during attack against Iran
entry.content:
The Jerusalem Post reports: As fighter jets and cruise missiles struck IRGC command centers, a parallel front reportedly paralyzed the Islamic Republic from within. Reports on Saturday, February 28, 2026, indicated that Iran entered an almost complete digital fog, in what appeared to be a large-scale cyberattack accompanying Operation “Roar of the Lion.” Critical infrastructure, official news sites,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Court Refuses to Slice Up CiCi’s Cyber Extortion Coverage
entry.content:
Andrea DeField and S. Alice Weeks of Hunton Andrews Kurth write: In the rarely litigated space of cyber insurance, the Northern District of Texas issued a win for cyber policyholders this week, offering a clear reminder to insurers that if they want to restrict coverage, they must draft the policy to clearly do so. In CiCi... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Connecticut Senate Bill Raises the Stakes on Data Breach Response
entry.content:
Hayley Steele and Gregory Szewczyk of Ballard Spahr write: A new bill introduced in Connecticut—Connecticut Senate Bill 117, An Act Concerning Breaches of Security Involving Electronic Personal Information—would create mandatory forensic examination requirements for entities that experience a “massive breach of security,” defined as a data breach affecting at least 100,000 Connecticut residents, and imposes... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.content:
Malaysia Airlines reported a ransomware. Attributed to Qilin.

entry.title: https://daricon.com/ (military US/CA/NATO)
